question: Describe the configuration of the NuScale control room.

nuscale_ch01_0852.title: SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0: Crediting Manual Operator Actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) AnalysesC.1.B Review Criteria for Phase 1 (Analysis)
nuscale_ch01_0852.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the crediting of manual operator actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) analyses, as outlined in SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0. It confirms that NuScale meets the review criteria for all phases of D3 analysis, with this appendix superseding previous guidance (DI&C ISG-05), while also noting that certain aspects of probabilistic risk assessment (PRA) remain the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0370.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.2 Environmental and Dynamic Effects
nuscale_ch01_0370.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Control Room Area Ventilation System, specifically regarding environmental and dynamic effects. It states that the system meets the requirements of SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3, with no exceptions noted for this criterion. The document also outlines compliance with related standards and clarifies that the control room habitability system uses clean compressed air rather than emergency filtration during accident conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0372.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.4 Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0372.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3 requirements for the Control Room Area Ventilation System. It confirms that the system meets regulatory criteria for protection against natural phenomena, environmental effects, and sharing of systems, with up to 12 modules sharing a control room. For radioactive material release control, NuScale uses compressed air tanks for clean air rather than emergency filtration during accident conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing reactor operators to monitor and control multiple NPMs using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive monitoring, alarms, and controls for plant operations, with additional display stations for shared systems and diagnostics.

nuscale_ch01_0853.title: SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0: Crediting Manual Operator Actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) AnalysesC.2.B Review Criteria for Phase 2 (Preliminary Validation)
nuscale_ch01_0853.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with NRC regulatory criteria regarding the crediting of manual operator actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) analyses, as outlined in SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0. It confirms that NuScale's approach aligns with the updated review criteria for all phases of D3 analysis, superseding previous guidance, while noting that certain aspects of probabilistic risk assessment remain the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0110.title: Figure 1.7-1b: Electrical Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0110.summary: This section provides a comprehensive legend of electrical symbols and abbreviations used in NuScale's Final Safety Analysis Report drawings, including representations for switches, relays, indicators, and logic gates. It also explains the meanings of various logic gate outputs and control room indications relevant to system status and alarms.

nuscale_ch01_0037.title: Control Building
nuscale_ch01_0037.summary: This section describes the refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM), which are performed independently for each module. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling area using specialized cranes and tools, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to operation after successful testing.

nuscale_ch01_0851.title: SRP 18.0, Rev 2: Human Factors EngineeringII.C Review of the HFE Aspects of Modifications Affecting Risk Important Human Actions
nuscale_ch01_0851.summary: This section states that the SRP 18.0, Rev 2 criteria for reviewing human factors engineering (HFE) aspects of modifications affecting risk-important human actions are not applicable to NuScale, as they pertain to existing reactor licensees. However, NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report conforms to the review criteria in SRP Appendix 18-A for crediting manual operator actions in diversity and defense-in-depth analyses, while site-specific hazard evaluations and PRA updates are the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0849.title: SRP 18.0, Rev 2: Human Factors EngineeringII.A Review of the HFE Aspects of a New Plant
nuscale_ch01_0849.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria related to Human Factors Engineering (HFE) as outlined in SRP 18.0, Rev 2. It states that NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report meets the requirements for reviewing HFE aspects of a new plant, with no exceptions noted, and references SRP sections 18.1 through 18.12 for further details. The section also clarifies that criteria for control room modifications are not applicable to new plants, but only to existing reactor licensees.

edges.description: Section B provides specific details about the control room area ventilation system, which is a component of the overall control room configuration referenced in the analysis question, while Section A addresses human factors and operator actions relevant to control room operation.

Section A addresses human factors engineering and operator actions in the NuScale control room, while Section B details the control room area ventilation system, with both sections connecting through their focus on aspects critical to the control room's configuration and safety functions.

Section B provides the physical and functional configuration details of the NuScale control room, which is the specific information required to answer the analysis question, while Section A discusses regulatory review criteria for human factors and operator actions but does not describe the control room configuration itself.

Section A outlines the review criteria for Phase 1 (analysis) of crediting manual operator actions in D3 analyses, while Section B provides the review criteria for Phase 2 (preliminary validation) of the same process, with both sections sequentially addressing different stages of the regulatory evaluation for the NuScale control room configuration.

Section B specifies regulatory requirements for the control room area ventilation system, which is a subsystem located within the control building described in Section A, thus Section B provides detailed criteria relevant to the configuration of the control room mentioned in Section A.

Section A references and defers to Section B by stating that SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0 supersedes the criteria in SRP 18.0, Rev 2 for the review of human factors engineering aspects related to manual operator actions in the NuScale control room.

Section B provides specific technical requirements and criteria for the control room area ventilation system, which is a key aspect of the overall control room configuration described in the analysis question, while Section A addresses human factors engineering considerations that may influence control room modifications but explicitly states non-applicability to the NuScale configuration.

Section B provides specific details about the control room area ventilation system, which is a component of the overall control room configuration described in the analysis question, while Section A addresses human factors engineering aspects that are not applicable to the NuScale control room configuration.

Section A outlines the general human factors engineering (HFE) review criteria for new plant control room configurations, while Section B provides specific review criteria for crediting manual operator actions in safety analyses, both referencing the NuScale control room's regulatory conformance.

Section A addresses human factors engineering and quality assurance aspects of the NuScale control room, while Section B details the control room area ventilation system, with both sections providing complementary information necessary to fully describe the control room configuration in terms of human factors and environmental controls.

Section A addresses human factors engineering and quality assurance aspects relevant to the control room, while Section B specifies the control room area ventilation system, with both sections providing complementary information necessary to fully describe the NuScale control room configuration.

Section A states that the review criteria for modifications affecting risk-important human actions are not applicable to NuScale as a new plant, while Section B provides the applicable human factors engineering review criteria for new plant configurations, directly informing the analysis of the NuScale control room configuration.
